name: Bad Cryptography
input:
In the first line of input there is single integer $t$ ($1 \le t \le 100$) — number of pairs, for which you need to find the discrete logarithm.
In each of next $t$ line there is a pair of integers $a$ $b$ ($1 \le a, b  \lt  2^{64}$).
output: For each pair you should print one integer $x$ ($0 \le x  \lt  2^{64}$), such that $a^{\odot x} = b$, or -1 if no such x exists. It can be shown, that if any such $x$ exists, there is one inside given bounds. If there are several good values, you can output any of them.
